name: transform
context: Transform pandas DataFrames with filters, selection, assign, rename, sort, reshape, pivot, melt, explode, map, and method chains
info: Transformation turns a cleaned DataFrame into the shape required for analysis, joins, tests, or export. Agents use this node for row filtering, column selection, renaming, vectorized computed columns, sorting, deduplication, reshaping between wide and long formats, exploding list-like cells, and building readable method chains. It emphasizes `.loc`, `.assign`, `.pipe`, vectorized `.str` and `.dt` accessors, and safe CLI transforms through the shared [[pandas/workflows]] script.
body:
# DataFrame Transformation

Transform after basic cleaning and before aggregation or export. Prefer readable method chains with vectorized operations.

## Method-chain example

```python
result = (
    df
    .rename(columns=str.lower)
    .loc[lambda d: d['amount'].gt(0)]
    .assign(
        domain=lambda d: d['email'].str.split('@').str[-1].str.lower(),
        month=lambda d: d['created_at'].dt.to_period('M').dt.to_timestamp(),
    )
    .sort_values(['month', 'amount'], ascending=[True, False])
)
```

## Reshaping

```python
long = wide.melt(id_vars=['account_id'], var_name='month', value_name='revenue')
wide_again = long.pivot(index='account_id', columns='month', values='revenue').reset_index()
exploded = df.explode('tags')
```

## CLI transform

```bash
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/transform_dataset.py raw.csv clean.parquet --select order_id,amount,status --query 'amount > 0' --drop-duplicates
```

sections.title: Filter and select
sections.body: Use boolean masks with `.loc` for explicit filtering: `df.loc[df['status'].eq('paid'), columns]`. `DataFrame.query()` is concise for simple expressions but should not receive untrusted user input. Use `filter`, `select_dtypes`, and column lists to constrain output shape.

sections.title: Assign computed columns
sections.body: Use `.assign(new=lambda d: ...)` inside chains so each computed column is visible to later lambdas. Prefer vectorized arithmetic, `.str`, `.dt`, `map`, `replace`, `where`, and `mask`. Avoid `axis=1` apply unless the dataset is small or the logic cannot be vectorized.

sections.title: Reshape
sections.body: `melt` converts wide columns into long rows. `pivot` and `pivot_table` convert long rows into wide tables. `stack` and `unstack` reshape MultiIndex objects. `explode` turns list-like cells into multiple rows. Choose shape based on the next operation: groupby often prefers long data; presentation often prefers wide data.

sections.title: Pipe reusable functions
sections.body: Use `.pipe(function, **kwargs)` to keep pipelines readable while moving domain logic into named functions. This makes cleaning and transformation code testable with [[testing]] and easier for agents to modify without breaking the sequence.

links.context: Section on computed columns and vectorized assignment
Clean and type data before transforming it

related.note: Transformation usually follows cleaning in a pipeline
Aggregations consume transformed columns and shapes
Pipeline functions should have fixture-based tests

scripts.name: Transform Dataset
scripts.description: Select, drop, rename, query, deduplicate, sort, and write a transformed dataset

depends.reason: Requires DataFrame selection and assignment semantics
